rationale: Builds on the current late projection EMA winner but pivots to a new idea category instead of another EMA-selection replay. Projection matrices now get tiny float row_gain vectors, and each late quant-aware snap renormalizes projection rows so magnitude lives mostly in those float controls while the int8-quantized weights stay row-equalized. That preserves the model function, compounds with the existing projection EMA/final snap path, adds only a small artifact payload, and directly targets the final int8 roundtrip score by making the highest-value matrices intrinsically easier to quantize.
title: Projection Row-Gain Equalized EMA Finalizer
